skill_targets.title: OrbitLabs Telemetry SDK
skill_targets.rationale: OrbitLabs' telemetry SDK is the primary public developer surface — it has >2,000 GitHub stars, active npm downloads, and comprehensive API docs. Most external developers interact with the company through this library.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: A skill built on this SDK would allow agents to instrument, query, and visualize telemetry pipelines autonomously, replacing a workflow that currently requires human expertise to navigate the SDK's event routing API.
skill_targets.existing_competition: Generic observability SDK guides; no OrbitLabs-specific agent skill exists

skill_targets.title: OrbitLabs Config Management API
skill_targets.rationale: The config API underpins environment promotion workflows used by external platform engineering teams. Strong adoption signal from GitHub integrations and third-party blog posts.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Agents with this skill can manage multi-environment config promotion end-to-end, removing a manual handoff step between dev and ops.
skill_targets.existing_competition: Generic REST config management patterns; Pulumi providers for config; no agent-ready skill

skill_targets.title: OrbitLabs Log Query Language
skill_targets.rationale: Proprietary query language for log analysis with moderate external adoption. Growing community interest but niche audience.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Agents fluent in OQL could answer complex observability questions without human query authoring.
skill_targets.existing_competition: PromQL, Splunk SPL guides; no OQL-specific skill

skill_targets.title: OrbitLabs Legacy Exporter
skill_targets.rationale: Deprecated export tool maintained for backward compatibility only. Minimal external usage.
skill_targets.differentiation_hypothesis: Narrow migration use case with no growth trajectory.
